hypotheses.title: LOX Collagen Crosslink Density Maps to Bond Occupation Probability, Creating a Sharp Percolation Phase Transition in T Cell Immune Exclusion
key_issues: Disordered fiber network may not map cleanly to lattice percolation
Wolf 2013 threshold is 4 um^2 cross-section not 3 um pore diameter (imprecise citation)
Intratumoral heterogeneity may smear sharp transition
citation_errors: Wolf 2013 threshold value imprecise (4 um^2 not 3 um)
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Field default assumption is gradual immune exclusion; no dose-response data shows sharp transition

hypotheses.title: Universal Critical Exponent nu = 0.88 Predicts T Cell Clustering Length Scale Across Tumor Types Independent of Molecular Details
key_issues: Active particles (Pe ~3) likely break isotropic percolation universality
Internal contradiction with H8 (which argues for directed percolation class)
Collagen alignment in desmoplastic tumors changes effective dimensionality
Requires tumors near p_c which cannot be guaranteed from clinical samples
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Smeared phase transitions in complex networks (Munoz 2018) modify apparent exponents; active particles change universality class

hypotheses.title: Subdiffusive MSD Exponent alpha = 0.53 at the Percolation Threshold Is a Universal Diagnostic Fingerprint for ECM-Mediated Immune Exclusion
key_issues: T cell run-and-pause behavior produces alpha ~ 0.5-0.7 without percolation (Krummel 2016)
Affirming-the-consequent: alpha = 0.53 from percolation does not mean alpha = 0.53 implies percolation
Riedl 2020 shows subdiffusion explained by two-state random walk without percolation
MSD exponent alone insufficient as diagnostic
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Riedl et al. 2020 (Biophys J) demonstrates T cell subdiffusion in collagen explained by two-state random walk model without percolation framework

hypotheses.title: BAPN Dose-Response Predicts a Sharp Nonlinear Phase Transition in Immune Infiltration
key_issues: Citation error: Tang 1983 cited as Tang 2017 (year wrong, mechanism correct)
p(dose) mapping function is unknown and potentially nonlinear
In vivo BAPN distribution heterogeneity may smear transition
citation_errors: Tang et al. 2017 should be Tang, Trackman & Kagan 1983 (J Biol Chem 258:4331)
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: No direct counter-evidence; unknown p(dose) mapping is the main vulnerability

hypotheses.title: Finite-Size Scaling Predicts That Biopsy Dimensions Systematically Bias Immune Exclusion Scoring
key_issues: FATAL: Predicted effect (0.6% shift) is 2-3 orders of magnitude smaller than intratumoral heterogeneity
Biopsy sampling variance dominated by tissue heterogeneity (Nature npj Precision Oncology 2021)
Percolation framing adds no practical insight beyond known biopsy sampling variance
hypotheses.kill_reason: Quantitative insufficiency: hypothesis's own math demonstrates the predicted effect is negligible relative to known confounders
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Nature npj Precision Oncology 2021: >5000 biopsies show within-biopsy variance dominated by tissue heterogeneity, not finite-size effects

hypotheses.title: MMP/LOX Kinetic Balance Creates Dynamic Percolation, Generating Temporal Windows of Immune Infiltration in Tumors
key_issues: Oversimplified kinetic model (>20 MMPs, 4 LOX family, TIMPs reduced to 2 rate constants)
LOX-crosslinked collagen may be resistant to MMP degradation, making ECM effectively static
Salmon 2012 attributes peri-tumoral trapping to fiber alignment not network connectivity
Multiple simpler explanations exist (chemokines, TGF-beta, fiber guidance)
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Salmon 2012 explicitly attributes peri-tumoral T cell trapping to collagen fiber alignment (geometric guidance), not network connectivity (percolation)

hypotheses.title: Percolation Backbone Fraction Predicts the Ratio of Functionally Cytotoxic to Trapped T Cells Within Infiltrated Tumors
key_issues: FATAL: T cell exhaustion is driven by chronic antigen stimulation (Wherry 2011), NOT physical confinement
Predicted direction of backbone-exhaustion correlation may be REVERSED
Trapped T cells with less antigen contact would be LESS exhausted not MORE
beta_B = 1.05 unverified via web search
hypotheses.kill_reason: Mechanism fundamentally contradicts established T cell exhaustion biology (Wherry 2011, Nat Immunol). Physical trapping does not cause exhaustion; chronic antigen stimulation does. The backbone-to-function mapping is biologically invalid.
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: Wherry 2011 (Nat Immunol 12:492-499): T cell exhaustion is driven by chronic TCR stimulation from persistent antigen, not physical confinement

hypotheses.title: Chemotaxis Breaks Percolation Universality -- Directed T Cell Migration Shifts the System to Directed Percolation with Distinct Exponents
key_issues: Complex multi-source chemokine gradients may not map to fixed-direction DP assumption
Pe ~ 3 sits in poorly characterized crossover regime
Creates internal inconsistency with H1-H4 exponent predictions
Practically difficult to test (requires co-staining + directional analysis)
hypotheses.strongest_counter_evidence: No direct counter-evidence; main concern is applicability of DP to complex gradient fields

critic_questions.question: Can you quantify how much intratumoral collagen density heterogeneity would smear the percolation transition?
Wolf 2013 reports arrest at 4 um^2 cross-section for T cells, not 3 um pore diameter. Please correct and assess impact.
If T cells are active particles with Pe ~ 3, what universality class does the system belong to?
What fraction of clinical tumors sit near p_c? Is the universality prediction practically testable?
What specific measurement distinguishes percolation subdiffusion from T cell run-and-pause subdiffusion?
Has anyone measured T cell velocity autocorrelation in collagen with sufficient precision?
Tang citation should be 1983 not 2017. Please correct.
Can you estimate the shape of p(dose) for BAPN to assess whether percolation signature survives?
Can you distinguish dynamic percolation from Salmon 2012 fiber alignment explanation experimentally?
What is the degradation timescale of LOX-crosslinked collagen specifically?
If chemokine gradient varies on correlation length scales, does the system revert to isotropic percolation?
Can you propose a simpler observable than anisotropic correlation lengths?

meta_critique.kill_rate_assessment: Within acceptable range (target 30-50%), below ideal lower bound. Kills are justified by quantitative insufficiency (H5) and biological contradiction (H7).
meta_critique.systemic_risk: All hypotheses depend on lattice abstraction of disordered collagen fiber network. If this mapping is fundamentally invalid, all hypotheses fail together.
meta_critique.strongest_overall_counter_evidence: T cell run-and-pause behavior independently produces subdiffusion (Riedl 2020, Krummel 2016) and fiber alignment explains peri-tumoral trapping (Salmon 2012) -- both challenge the necessity of a percolation framework
